question: [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged maker whose offer has a callback under the precondition that the offer uses maxAssets-based accounting and is partially consumed trigger the sequence: (1) bundler calls consumableUnits to read consumed[maker][group] at snapshot S, (2) bundler calls take which fires maker's onBuy callback, (3) callback calls setConsumed(group, consumed+delta) on Midnight, (4) take completes and updates consumed again, causing consumed to exceed maxAssets, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity, causing scoped impact: accounting corruption where consumed[maker][group] exceeds offer.maxAssets without ConsumedAssets revert? Proof idea: fuzz delta in setConsumed callback and assert consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxAssets after bundle completes.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles loop] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that two offers in the takes[] array share the same offer.group and offer.maker trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits reads consumed[maker][group]=0 for offer[0], (2) take(offer[0], unitsToTake0) updates consumed to X, (3) consumableUnits reads consumed[maker][group]=X for offer[1] and computes remaining=maxUnits-X, (4) take(offer[1], remaining) updates consumed to maxUnits, violating the invariant that group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: total fill across group equals exactly maxUnits but the second consumableUnits snapshot is stale if a callback between steps 2 and 3 further modifies consumed? Proof idea: stateful fuzz with callback that calls setConsumed between takes and assert sum of filled units <= offer.maxUnits.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits (maxAssets branch) / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxAssets > 0 and offer.buy=false (seller offer) trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits calls sellerAssetsToUnits(midnight, id, offer, offer.maxAssets.zeroFloorSub(consumed)) which uses mulDivDown rounding, (2) take uses mulDivUp(buyerPrice, WAD) for buyerAssets and mulDivUp(sellerPrice, WAD) for sellerAssets, (3) the rounding divergence causes unitsToTake to yield sellerAssets > offer.maxAssets - consumed, (4) take reverts with ConsumedAssets but bundler try/catch silently swallows it, violating the invariant that filledUnits must equal targetUnits, causing scoped impact: OutOfOffers revert even though sufficient offer capacity exists, freezing the bundle? Proof idea: fuzz tick and maxAssets near rounding boundaries and assert take does not revert when consumableUnits returns nonzero.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits (maxAssets branch) / TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.buy=true and offer.maxAssets > 0 trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits calls buyerAssetsToUnits with targetBuyerAssets = offer.maxAssets - consumed, using mulDivUp(WAD, buyerPrice), (2) take computes buyerAssets = units.mulDivDown(buyerPrice, WAD), (3) the mulDivUp/mulDivDown asymmetry causes actual buyerAssets < offer.maxAssets - consumed, (4) consumed[maker][group] += buyerAssets leaves residual capacity, (5) next consumableUnits call returns nonzero units again, allowing the offer to be re-entered in the same bundle loop, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity, causing scoped impact: offer overfill by repeated partial fills within one bundle call? Proof idea: unit test with tick=0 (price near 1) and maxAssets=1 and assert consumed never exceeds maxAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / settlementFee staleness] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that block.timestamp is within 1 second of a settlement fee breakpoint boundary trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits calls TakeAmountsLib which calls settlementFee(id, zeroFloorSub(maturity, block.timestamp)) at time T, (2) block advances to T+1 crossing a breakpoint, (3) take calls settlementFee at T+1 with a different fee, (4) the units computed by consumableUnits yield buyerAssets that exceed offer.maxAssets at the new fee, (5) take reverts with ConsumedAssets and bundler silently skips, violating the invariant that filledUnits must equal targetUnits, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS at fee breakpoint boundaries? Proof idea: fuzz block.timestamp near breakpoints [0,1,7,30,90,180,360] days before maturity and assert bundle completes.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / zeroFloorSub edge case] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that consumed[maker][group] >= offer.maxAssets (offer fully consumed) trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits returns 0 via zeroFloorSub, (2) bundler calls take(offer, 0 units), (3) Midnight allows a no-op take with 0 units and fires the maker callback, (4) callback executes arbitrary state changes including withdrawCollateral or setConsumed, violating the invariant that callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: callback-driven state corruption triggered by a zero-unit take on a fully consumed offer? Proof idea: unit test with consumed=maxUnits and assert take(0) callback cannot alter borrower health or consumed state.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the maker's onSell callback reenters MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget with a different takes[] array trigger the sequence: (1) outer bundle calls consumableUnits for offer[0] and calls take, (2) onSell callback fires and calls supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget again, (3) inner bundle calls consumableUnits for offer[0] again reading the same consumed value (take not yet returned), (4) inner bundle fills offer[0] again, (5) outer take returns and increments consumed a second time, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity, causing scoped impact: double-fill of offer[0] within one transaction? Proof idea: PoC with reentrant onSell callback and assert consumed[maker][group] <= offer.maxUnits after both calls.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that referralFeePct > 0 and the maker's onBuy callback reenters buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral trigger the sequence: (1) outer bundle computes targetFilledBuyerAssets = targetBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets, (2) consumableUnits returns units U for offer[0], (3) take fires onBuy callback which reenters the bundle with the same takes[], (4) inner bundle fills offers consuming filledBuyerAssets2, (5) outer bundle continues accumulating filledBuyerAssets on top, (6) final check filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets passes but total assets pulled from msg.sender is 2*targetBuyerAssets, violating the invariant that total loan assets transferred from msg.sender equals targetBuyerAssets, causing scoped impact: double-spend of msg.sender loan tokens? Proof idea: PoC with reentrant onBuy and assert msg.sender token balance decreases by exactly targetBuyerAssets.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.repayAndWithdrawCollateral] Can an unprivileged borrower under the precondition that the repay callback reenters withdrawCollateral before repay's debt reduction is finalized trigger the sequence: (1) repayAndWithdrawCollateral calls repay(market, units, onBehalf, callback), (2) repay decrements debt and increments withdrawable, then fires callback, (3) callback calls withdrawCollateral on behalf of onBehalf, (4) isHealthy check passes because debt was already decremented, (5) collateral is withdrawn, (6) repay then pulls loan tokens from payer, violating the invariant that collateral cannot be withdrawn if it would make debt unhealthy, causing scoped impact: collateral withdrawal that would have been blocked pre-repay is now unblocked via callback sequencing? Proof idea: fuzz collateral amount and assert isHealthy holds for original debt before repay completes.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles loop try/catch] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that a malicious maker deploys a callback that reverts selectively trigger the sequence: (1) bundler calls consumableUnits returning nonzero units, (2) bundler calls take which fires maker callback, (3) callback reverts, (4) take reverts with WrongBuyCallbackReturnValue, (5) bundler try/catch swallows the revert, (6) filledUnits is not incremented, (7) loop continues to next offer, (8) all offers in takes[] are exhausted without filling targetUnits, (9) OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that valid offers with sufficient capacity must be fillable, causing scoped impact: low-cost DoS of bundle by maker deploying a selectively reverting callback? Proof idea: unit test with callback that reverts on first call and assert bundle reverts with OutOfOffers.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits (maxUnits=0, maxAssets=0 branch)] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that offer.maxUnits=0 and offer.maxAssets=0 trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits checks maxUnits > 0 (false), then checks offer.buy, (2) if offer.buy=true calls buyerAssetsToUnits(midnight, id, offer, zeroFloorSub(0, consumed)) = buyerAssetsToUnits(..., 0) which returns 0, (3) bundler calls take(offer, 0), (4) Midnight allows no-op take and fires callback, (5) callback executes arbitrary actions, violating the invariant that callbacks must not corrupt partial state through reentrancy, causing scoped impact: callback execution on an offer with no capacity limit, enabling unbounded callback-driven state changes? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / cross-market group budget] Can an unprivileged maker under the precondition that two offers in different markets share the same offer.group and offer.maker trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits for market A reads consumed[maker][group]=0 and returns maxUnits, (2) take in market A updates consumed[maker][group]=maxUnits, (3) consumableUnits for market B reads consumed[maker][group]=maxUnits and returns 0, (4) bundler skips market B offer, (5) but if the bundle only checks one market's id, a cross-market group exhaustion is silently accepted, violating the invariant that consumption group budget cannot be exceeded across offers, causing scoped impact: group budget correctly enforced cross-market but bundler silently skips valid offers, causing OutOfOffers DoS? Proof idea: fuzz two markets sharing a group and assert bundle behavior is consistent.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.withdrawCollateral post-bundle] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the bundle fills sell offers increasing taker's debt and then calls withdrawCollateral trigger the sequence: (1) supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget fills takes increasing taker debt, (2) collateralWithdrawals loop calls withdrawCollateral for taker, (3) isHealthy is checked with the new debt, (4) if consumableUnits overestimated units due to rounding, actual debt is higher than expected, (5) withdrawCollateral reverts with UnhealthyBorrower, violating the invariant that the bundle must complete atomically, causing scoped impact: partial bundle execution where debt is increased but collateral withdrawal fails, leaving taker in a worse position? Proof idea: fuzz units near health threshold and assert withdrawCollateral succeeds after bundle fills.

[File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / liquidation callback reentrancy] Can an unprivileged liquidator under the precondition that the liquidation callback reenters MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral trigger the sequence: (1) liquidate fires onLiquidate callback, (2) callback calls buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral, (3) bundle calls consumableUnits reading consumed at snapshot S, (4) bundle calls take which updates consumed, (5) liquidation completes updating borrower debt and collateral, (6) bundle's subsequent withdrawCollateral uses stale health state, violating the invariant that multicall/callback/reentrancy must not break invariants that hold for single calls, causing scoped impact: collateral withdrawal succeeding post-liquidation that would fail in isolation? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / fee claim reentrancy] Can an unprivileged fee claimer under the precondition that claimContinuousFee reduces totalUnits and withdrawable trigger the sequence: (1) bundle calls touchMarket to snapshot settlement fees, (2) fee claimer calls claimContinuousFee reducing withdrawable, (3) bundle calls take which increments withdrawable via sellerCreditDecrease, (4) consumableUnits was computed before claimContinuousFee so units are based on pre-claim state, (5) actual withdrawable after take is lower than expected, violating the invariant that contract token balances must cover withdrawable assets, causing scoped impact: withdrawable accounting desync after concurrent fee claim and bundle take? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithAssetsTarget referralFee] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that referralFeePct is near WAD-1 trigger the sequence: (1) referralFeeAssets = targetSellerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD-referralFeePct) overflows or produces a very large value, (2) targetFilledSellerAssets = targetSellerAssets + referralFeeAssets wraps or is huge, (3) consumableUnits is called with a huge remaining target, (4) sellerAssetsToUnits returns a huge units value, (5) take reverts with ConsumedUnits, (6) try/catch swallows, (7) OutOfOffers revert, violating the invariant that referralFeePct < WAD check is sufficient to prevent overflow, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS via referralFeePct near WAD? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithAssetsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral filledBuyerAssets check] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that the last take in the loop fills exactly targetFilledBuyerAssets but consumableUnits returned more units than needed trigger the sequence: (1) consumableUnits returns U units for the last offer, (2) min(TakeAmountsLib.buyerAssetsToUnits(..., remaining), takes[i].units, U) selects units V < U, (3) take(V) yields buyerAssets that overshoots targetFilledBuyerAssets by rounding, (4) filledBuyerAssets > targetFilledBuyerAssets, (5) require(filledBuyerAssets == targetFilledBuyerAssets) reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that the bundle must complete when sufficient offer capacity exists, causing scoped impact: bundle DoS due to rounding overshoot in buyerAssets accumulation? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles authorization check] Can an unprivileged msg.sender under the precondition that taker has authorized the bundler but not msg.sender trigger the sequence: (1) bundler checks isAuthorized(taker, msg.sender) which is false, (2) bundler reverts with Unauthorized, (3) but if msg.sender == taker the check passes, (4) msg.sender calls bundle on behalf of taker using taker's authorization of bundler, (5) consumableUnits reads consumed[maker][group] for offers that taker is not the maker of, (6) take proceeds with taker as seller increasing taker's debt, violating the invariant that authorization must only allow intended account delegation, causing scoped impact: unauthorized debt increase for taker via bundler authorization confusion? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral collateralWithdrawals] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that collateralWithdrawals[] references a collateral index not in taker's collateralBitmap trigger the sequence: (1) bundle fills takes increasing taker's credit (buy side), (2) collateralWithdrawals loop calls withdrawCollateral for collateralIndex not activated, (3) position.collateral[collateralIndex] = 0, withdrawal of 0 assets is a no-op, (4) but if assets > 0 the subtraction underflows with uint128 underflow, (5) revert propagates out of bundle, violating the invariant that the bundle must complete atomically, causing scoped impact: bundle revert due to collateral index mismatch leaving taker with increased credit but no collateral withdrawal? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles forceApproveMax reentrancy] Can an unprivileged attacker under the precondition that the loan token is an ERC777-like token with transfer hooks trigger the sequence: (1) pullToken calls safeTransferFrom which fires a token hook, (2) hook reenters MidnightBundles before forceApproveMax is called, (3) reentrant call calls consumableUnits reading consumed at snapshot S, (4) reentrant take fills offers, (5) outer call continues with forceApproveMax and fills the same offers again, (6) consumed exceeds maxUnits, violating the invariant that offer fill amount cannot exceed remaining offer capacity, causing scoped impact: offer overfill via ERC777 hook reentrancy into bundle before approval? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.supplyCollateralAndSellWithUnitsTarget filledUnits check] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that consumableUnits returns 0 for all offers in takes[] (all fully consumed) trigger the sequence: (1) loop calls consumableUnits for each offer returning 0, (2) min(targetUnits - filledUnits, takes[i].units, 0) = 0, (3) take(0 units) is called for each offer, (4) each take is a no-op but may fire callbacks, (5) filledUnits remains 0, (6) require(filledUnits == targetUnits) reverts with OutOfOffers, violating the invariant that the bundle must not fire callbacks on fully consumed offers, causing scoped impact: callback execution on fully consumed offers causing state changes before OutOfOffers revert? [File: 'src/periphery/ConsumableUnitsLib.sol -> Scope: Critical Reentrancy or callback-driven state corruption across take, repay, withdraw, liquidation, fee claim, or collateral flows'] [Function: consumableUnits / MidnightBundles.buyWithUnitsTargetAndWithdrawCollateral remaining token transfer] Can an unprivileged taker under the precondition that filledBuyerAssets < maxBuyerAssets and referralFeeAssets > 0 trigger the sequence: (1) bundle fills takes with filledBuyerAssets, (2) referralFeeAssets = filledBuyerAssets.mulDivDown(referralFeePct, WAD - referralFeePct), (3) remaining = maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets, (4) if referralFeeAssets > maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets the subtraction underflows, (5) safeTransfer reverts, violating the invariant that msg.sender receives back exactly maxBuyerAssets - filledBuyerAssets - referralFeeAssets, causing scoped impact: bundle revert with underflow leaving loan tokens locked in bundler?
